description_long: This is a tall, narrow radiator with a series of evenly spaced vertical columns, likely made of metal, mounted on a flat rectangular backplate. The radiator features visible pipe connections and valve mechanisms at the bottom, suggesting it is designed for wall mounting. The vertical columns are tubular and run the full height of the radiator, providing a large surface area for heat dissipation. The finish appears smooth and metallic, with a warm, bronze-like color dominating the visible surfaces. There are no decorative grilles or covers, and the design is minimalist and functional. The overall construction suggests durability and efficient heat transfer, suitable for modern urban interiors or utility spaces.
description: A tall, narrow, wall-mounted radiator with vertical metal columns and visible pipe connections at the bottom. It has a smooth, bronze-like finish and a minimalist, functional design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full height of the radiator, vertical columns, and visible pipe connections and valves at the bottom.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the radiator's slim profile and the protrusion of the columns from the wall-mounted backplate.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the flat backplate with no visible columns or pipework, emphasizing the mounting surface.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again highlighting the slim profile and the depth of the vertical columns relative to the wall.
attribute_pipe_connections: two pipe connections at the bottom left, horizontally oriented
